figures.caption: Figure 2: CHSH win rate versus training steps with std dev bands. (a) Effect of each of the four Bell states (|Φ−⟩, |Φ+⟩, |Ψ−⟩, |Ψ+⟩). (b) Win rate by input pair for all five QMARL variants. The horizontal lines mark the proven classical bound (0.75) and the Tsirelson bound (0.854).
Figure 9: CoopNav: Qubit count and circuit depth ablation. Shaded bands show std dev across 10 seeds.
Figure 1: QMARL under CTDE: Agents execute decentralised quantum policies independently; a centralised critic trains all agents using global state and joint actions.
Figure 3: CoinGame: Classical MARL versus QMARL without entanglement (top) and across Bell states (bottom).
Figure 8: CoinGame: Qubit count and circuit depth ablation (no entanglement). Shaded bands show std dev across 10 seeds.

Figure 7: Effect of entropy regularisation per entanglement type. Win rate versus training steps for β = 0.0 (blue) versus β = 0.2 (orange dashed) for each Bell state separately: a) |Φ−⟩, b) |Φ+⟩, c) |Ψ−⟩, d) |Ψ+⟩. The solid black horizontal lines mark the classical bound (0.75) and the Tsirelson bound (0.854). Shaded bands show std dev across seeds.
Figure 4: CoopNav: Effect of entanglement variant on success rate, collision count and episode length.
Figure 5: CoopNav: Effect of quantum hybridizations on success rate, collision count and episode length.

Table 8: Trainable parameter counts for all policy architectures. Classical: fully connected Dense layers, params = nin × nout + nout per layer. Quantum actor: three components, (i) preprocessing Dense(nobs → nq×3) [dominant cost], (ii) PQC variational+encoding layers [compact],(iii) readout. Bell state preparation is a fixed non-parametric resource with zero trainable parameters. The centralised critic is identical across classical and quantum variants.

Figure 6: Effect of entropy regularisation. Left panel shows win rate curves for all eight combinations of entanglement state and entropy coefficient (β ∈ {0.0, 0.2}). Right panel aggregates win rate averaged over all four entangled variants at β = 0.0 (blue) versus β = 0.2 (orange dashed).
Table 6: CoopNav full ablation: success rate across all actor/critic/qubit/depth configurations (5×5 grid, pslip = 0.10, 10 seeds).
